title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots among teams watching Maxx Crosby trade situation as Raiders receive interest from multiple franchises.
NFL's Albert Breer suggests a trade for Crosby could occur this week as teams clarify financial statuses.
Crosby, 29, desires to win; Raiders coming off a 3-14 season and holding the No. 1 draft pick.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Quincy man Sanusi Sadiq sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in St. Patrick's Day assault.
Victim Barry Whelan died from blunt force trauma after being assaulted outside an ATM in 2023.
Sadiq apologized for the incident, citing poor judgement; a previous trial ended in a mistrial.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons predicts Jayson Tatum will return this Friday against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
The Celtics have not confirmed a return date for Tatum, who is recovering from achilles tendon repair.
Tatum has been cleared for full practice; Celtics have won 40 games, eight out of the last ten, without him.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court to review law limiting handgun licenses to those 21 and older.
The case stems from the 2021 arrest of Mikai Thomson, who was convicted of illegal firearm possession at age 20.
Gun-rights advocates argue age limits infringe on Second Amendment rights post-2022 Supreme Court ruling.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney aims to improve the team without sacrificing long-term plans ahead of Friday's trade deadline.
Sweeney is cautious about acquiring rental players and prefers long-term impact players to bolster the roster.
Despite past asset sacrifices, Sweeney remains focused on playoff contention while maintaining future flexibility.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo set to return against Celtics while Jaylen Brown is out with an illness
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is expected to return against the Celtics after recovering from a right calf strain.
Jaylen Brown and Neemias Queta will miss the Celtics' game against the Bucks due to illness and rest, respectively.
The Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference, trailing 5.5 games behind first-place Detroit.

title: Citing ‘wokeness,’ Pentagon cancels military fellowships at New England schools like MIT and Tufts
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Pentagon ended military fellowships at schools like MIT, Tufts, and Brown, citing 'leftist ideology' issues.
Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth criticized these institutions for fostering 'anti-American resentment' with taxpayer dollars.
93 fellowships have been canceled across 22 institutions, with new partners like University of Michigan considered for future programs.

title: Rescued pocket-sized owl turning heads in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A saw-whet owl, the smallest owl species in eastern U.S., was found injured in a New England driveway.
The owl, approximately 6.7 to 8.3 inches tall, was photographed next to a banana for scale by Newhouse Wildlife Rescue.
Newhouse transferred the owl to Cape Ann Wildlife for treatment of two broken bones and launched a related fundraiser.

title: Massachusetts joins insurers cutting coverage for popular weight-loss drugs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts Group Insurance Commission voted 10-7 to end GLP-1 weight loss drug coverage for state employees.
Current GLP-1 drug coverage costs the state $46 million annually, affecting about 22,000 members.
The decision aims to save $100 million, aligning with Gov. Healey's push to control rising drug expenses.

title: After Columbia incident, Healey and Campbell remind schools that ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts officials remind schools ICE agents need a judicial warrant to enter dorms after Columbia incident.
Gov. Healey and AG Campbell issued guidance on refusing entry to ICE without a warrant at educational institutions.
Healey's executive order bars ICE from making arrests in non-public areas and using state property for operations.
